description: Create a fully-constrained 2D wireframe of a bicycle frame using 4 points and 5 line segments. Point B (Bottom Bracket) must be coincident with the origin (0,0), and line AB (Chainstay) must be horizontal with Point A positioned to the left of Point B. The frame is formed by lines BC (Seat Tube), CD (Top Tube), BD (Down Tube), and AC (Seatstay). Constrain the geometry with lengths AB = 430 mm, BC = 450 mm, and CD = 600 mm, and interior angles A-B-C = 107° and A-B-D = 135°. Ensure points C and D are located at positive Y coordinates. Save the solved file to /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/bike_wireframe.slvs.
